DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF DAVID CRUZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS AND FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS: UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.: THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FOURTH CATEGORY (SEE OPP. 5:27-28). THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CATEGORIES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE THIRD CATEGORY (SEE REPLY 9:12-13). PLAINTIFF'S COMPENSATION FROM THIS THIRD PARTY IS RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES. WOODLAND PARK APARTMENTS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE SUBPOENA IS QUASHED IN ITS ENTIRETY. SUBSEQUENT TO THE SUBPOENA, PLAINTIFF RESPONDED TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 43. (REPLY 6:18-20). WELLS FARGO: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE SUBPOENA IS QUASHED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT ARE NOT REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. HOWARD RAGLAND, MD: THE MOTION IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS. THE SUBPOENA IS LIMITED TO TREATMENT FOR THE INJURIES PLAINTIFF HAS PUT AT ISSUE IN THIS ACTION. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpopenas And For Award Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Mar-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Pia Kim, Esq. 415-762-3100 of Nassiri Jung for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DAVID CRUZ's Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Award Of Attorney'S Fees.

The Motion to Quash Subpoenas is GRANTED and DENIED as follows:

Uber Technologies, Inc.: The Motion is GRANTED as to the fourth category (see Opp. 5:27-28). The Motion is GRANTED as to the first and second categories. The Motion is DENIED as to the third category (See Reply 9:12-13). Plaintiff's compensation from this third party is relevant to plaintiff's damages.

Woodland Park Apartments: The Motion is GRANTED. The subpoena is quashed in its entirety. Subsequent to the subpoena, plaintiff responded to Special Interrogatory 43. (Reply 6:18-20).

Wells Fargo: The Motion is GRANTED. The subpoena is quashed in its entirety. The documents sought are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence.

Howard Ragland, MD: The Motion is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The Subpoena is limited to treatment for the injuries plaintiff has put at issue in this action.

The request for sanctions is denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
